content:
- Ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, which refreshed `.ralph/current_tasks.md`, `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, and task history, and sent the Ralph switch notification email.
- Committed all remaining task-closeout changes as `79072b2` with the required `task finished 04-task-migrate-ci-to-nix-only: ...` message including local and hosted verification evidence.
- Next: finish the required `git push`; if it succeeds, the turn is complete and I should quit immediately.

- Read previous progress, refreshed the Ralph task summaries, and inspected the active failing tasks plus the completed-task summary.
- Determined the actual repo stack from code: Rust workspace (`crates/runner`, `ingest-contract`, `operator-log`) with Nix `flake.nix` and `crane` as the primary build/test/lint/image path; also a small Go `molt` module. Confirmed there are no active unblocked failing bug tasks and no `<blocked_by>` or `<priority>` tags on the three active failing candidates.
- Chose the story-29 image-boundary smell task as next because it removes remaining legacy Dockerfile/workflow/test coupling exposed by the Nix image migration and should happen before adding the dev-only Docker fallback in story 29 task 05. Next, if quitting due to context, continue from `.ralph/tasks/smells/2026-04-28-story-29-nix-image-generation-boundaries.md`.

- Read previous progress, current task pointer, and the on-disk `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/smells/2026-04-28-story-29-nix-image-generation-boundaries.md`; next step is to inspect the task body and determine whether to create/verify a plan or execute it.

- Read the active smell task body and confirmed it is still `not_started`, so this turn must create a plan and stop instead of implementing.
- Inspected `flake.nix`, `publish-images.yml`, the Nix image artifact harness, and the runner/verify artifact/runtime test harnesses to map the real image boundary seams before writing the plan.

- Read the public runner/verify image contract tests and the novice registry-only contract to anchor the future RED slices to public behavior instead of implementation details.
- Noted that the smell text still references a dead `RustWorkspaceImageCacheContract`; the next step is to verify whether any Dockerfile-shaped helper survives and then write the smell follow-up plan under the story-29 task-03 plan directory.

- Created the smell follow-up plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-29-migrate-to-nix-crane/03-task-migrate-docker-image-generation-to-nix_plans/2026-04-28-nix-image-generation-boundaries-follow-up-plan.md`.
- Updated the active smell task `.ralph/tasks/smells/2026-04-28-story-29-nix-image-generation-boundaries.md` to `in_progress` and added the `<plan>` pointer.
- The plan ends in `TO BE VERIFIED` and is execution-ready for the next turn once the interface choices are accepted.
- Important discovery for the next turn: the smell's `RustWorkspaceImageCacheContract` reference is stale because that file is already gone; the real remaining work is consolidating shared Nix image identity and Docker lifecycle support across the runner/verify/novice harnesses.
- Next step after quitting: read the new plan, verify the design still holds, switch it to `NOW EXECUTE` if satisfied, and then stop immediately per the task rules.

- Read the smell task pointer and both required skill files from disk.
- Confirmed the previous turn created a follow-up plan for the active smell task and that this turn must inspect the task body plus plan state before doing any code edits.
- Next: read the smell task and its linked plan, then decide whether to keep it in verification/planning or switch it to execution and quit immediately if required.

- Read the active smell task, its linked follow-up plan, and the live Nix image support files to re-verify the design before execution.
- Confirmed the plan still matches the code: shared Nix provisioning already exists, the stale Dockerfile/cache helper is gone, and the remaining work is the duplicated image identity plus Docker lifecycle plumbing across runner/verify/novice harnesses.
- Switched `.ralph/tasks/story-29-migrate-to-nix-crane/03-task-migrate-docker-image-generation-to-nix_plans/2026-04-28-nix-image-generation-boundaries-follow-up-plan.md` from `TO BE VERIFIED` to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next step after quitting immediately: start the execution turn, read the `tdd` skill again, and implement the vertical red-green slices from that plan without changing the design unless execution proves it wrong.

- Read the active smell task, the linked follow-up execution plan, and the relevant improve-code-boundaries smell docs for smells 3, 10, and 13.
- Confirmed the task is in `NOW EXECUTE` and that the plan targets shared Nix image identity plus shared Docker image lifecycle plumbing across runner/verify/novice test support.
- Next step: inspect the live runner test-support files and pick the first small red-green slice that centralizes image identity without weakening the public image-contract tests.

- Baseline runner image contract passed, then I made the first red slice by switching `runner_image_artifact_harness.rs` to `NixImageArtifact::runner()` before that constructor existed.
- Added shared named image constructors `runner()` and `verify()` in `nix_image_artifact_harness.rs`, turning the focused runner image-contract slice green again.
- Refactored the remaining runner/verify/novice support call sites (`runner_image_harness`, `runner_container_process`, `verify_image_artifact_harness`, `verify_image_harness`, `published_image_refs`) to consume the shared named image specs instead of open-coded package/tag identity.
- Next step: validate the shared identity boundary with focused public image-contract tests, then extract the duplicated Docker create/export/cp/rm lifecycle helper into shared support.

- Centralized Nix image identity behind named shared constructors and replaced open-coded runner/verify/novice image references across the runner test support files.
- Extracted the repeated Docker temporary-container lifecycle into a shared support file used by the runner/verify image artifact harnesses, while keeping explicit failure contexts and no swallowed cleanup errors.
- Verified focused public contracts during red-green work: runner image build/filesystem tests, verify image build/filesystem/module tests, and the novice copied compose contract all passed.
- `make check` now passes after staging the new support file and tightening the integration-test dead-code handling; `make lint` has passed and `make test` is currently running.
- Next step if the context ends: wait for `make test`, then update the smell task checkboxes and `<passes>`, run `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, commit all tracked changes including `.ralph`, push, and quit immediately.
